ground_truth_summary: Marker drawing of a Spotify-themed cassette tape. Magnetic tape unspools from bottom and flows into abstract green/teal watercolor-like waves. French handwritten text 'MUSIQUE À VOLONTÉ' in upper right, with hand-drawn arrow pointing to the cassette. White background, black ink + green/teal/lime watercolor-marker washes.

key_hits: Correctly captures French text 'MUSIQUE À VOLONTÉ' with proper accent on É
Identifies cassette tape as central subject with reels and clear window
Magnetic tape unspooling into abstract waves
Correctly identifies lime green + forest green/teal palette
Notes arrow pointing from text toward cassette
White background, mixed ink + watercolor/marker style
Provides translation of the French phrase
key_misses: Says cassette is 'facing downward toward the right' / tilted slightly — actual orientation has cassette in upper-left with tape flowing downward-right (minor)
Calls it 'compact cassette' rather than the Spotify-themed detail (Spotify branding not visible in this marker drawing anyway)
judgments.justification: Excellent all-around: captures the rare French OCR with proper accent, accurate palette, correct identification of mixed media (ink + watercolor/marker), and notes the arrow. Minor imprecision on cassette orientation but composition is essentially right.

key_hits: Captures French text 'MUSIQUE À VOLONTÉ' with accent
Identifies green/chartreuse and teal color palette
White background
Provides translation of French phrase
key_misses: Describes cassette as a 'Cassette Player/Recorder' with 'translucent front panel' — wrong object (it is just a cassette tape, not a player)
Says the text appears 'twice' on the image — it appears only once
Describes 'a thick black winding cord' descending from the cassette — this is the unspooling magnetic tape, misidentified
Misses the arrow detail
hallucinations: 'Cassette Player/Recorder' rather than a bare cassette tape
judgments.justification: Despite getting the rare French OCR right, this response badly misidentifies the central object (calls a cassette tape a 'player/recorder'), fabricates a second occurrence of the text, and mislabels the magnetic tape as a 'cord/cable'. Major object-recognition failures drag the score down.

key_hits: Captures the two key French words 'MUSIQUE' and 'À VOLONTE' (dropped accent on final É)
Identifies audio cassette with reels, slider, and buttons
Magnetic tape flowing in wavy loops
Green and blue/teal watercolor washes creating movement
White background, hand-drawn ink/watercolor style
Notes arrow from text down to cassette
Provides translation
key_misses: Writes 'VOLONTE' without accent (should be 'VOLONTÉ')
Says cassette has 'control buttons (e.g., play/pause, stop)' — those are part of cassette body detail but a minor overstatement
Cassette colors listed as 'shades of gray' (body is more green-tinted in the artwork)
judgments.justification: Solid response that captures both French words and identifies the main composition, arrow, and color palette. Loses points for dropping the accent on VOLONTÉ (key fact: French OCR accuracy is rewarded) and slightly overstating the cassette's button detail.

Identifies audio cassette with transparent shell, two spools, control panel
Magnetic tape shown as flowing, coiled mass
Arrow connecting text to cassette explicitly noted

Notes the medium as ink + watercolor/marker
Provides translation and notes the symbolic meaning
key_misses: Says background is 'light off-white' rather than pure white (minor — image background is essentially white)
judgments.justification: Excellent response. Verbatim French OCR with proper accent, well-organized description, accurate palette and medium, and explicitly notes the arrow. Only the most minor off-white vs white quibble.

judgments.justification: Strong, concise response with verbatim French OCR (accent preserved), correct cassette description with the green stripe detail, and accurate palette. Minor over-reading of cassette internal detail keeps it just below the top.

judgments.justification: Captures the rare French OCR perfectly with accent, describes the abstract wave transformation well, and nails the palette. Minor over-reading of fine cassette hardware details (latches, screws) that aren't really visible in this hand-drawn image.

key_hits: Correctly captures French text 'MUSIQUE À VOLONTÉ' verbatim with accent on É, formatted as two lines
Cassette with clear plastic casing, lime-green strip across top, two spools, magnetic tape visible

Only minor deduction for the creative 'hand with fingers' interpretation of the abstract wave shape.
